item.mc_talking.talking_device.tooltip: Left-click on a citizen to start talking with them
mc_talking.configuration.title: MineColonies Talking Configuration
mc_talking.too_far: Your conversation has been ended - You are too far away.
mc_talking.no_key: No Gemini API key set. Please set one in the config file of MineColonies Talking. Until then, the mod will not work.
mc_talking.recruit_lore: Maybe this little gift will help you get this citizen back to join your colony.
mc_talking.invalid_on_visitor: You cannot use the Talking Device on visitors.
mc_talking.commands.tool_description:
The description of tool %s is:
%s
Properties:%s
mc_talking.debug.not_citizen: Target must be a MineColonies citizen.
mc_talking.debug.no_colony_specified: Specify a colony ID or execute near a colony.
mc_talking.debug.no_colony_found: Could not find a colony for you. Specify a colony ID.
mc_talking.debug.memory_disabled: Citizen memory is disabled in config.
mc_talking.debug.memory_empty: No memories stored for this citizen.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.geminiApi: Gemini API key used to authenticate all AI requests. If empty, all functionality is disabled.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.currentAi: Model used for live Gemini WebSocket conversations.
Preferred response language. If players ask to speak in another language, the AI will for their session.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.respondIn: If disabled, citizens ignore your microphone while you are in a voice chat group.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.continueW: When enabled, citizens will continue wandering normally while talking to a player. When disabled (default), they stand still for the duration of the conversation.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.sendMumbl: If enabled, text messages from mumbling and citizen-to-citizen conversations will also be sent to nearby players in chat.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.maxConcur: Maximum number of simultaneous citizen AI sessions. Player conversations have priority and can evict non-player sessions when full.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.maxConver: If you move farther than this many blocks from your active citizen, the player conversation ends automatically.
Output mode for normal live sessions: TEXT, AUDIO, or TEXT_AND_AUDIO.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.sendError: If enabled, players with OP level receive Gemini runtime errors in chat; otherwise errors are only logged.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.disabledT: Function-call tool names to block from the AI. Disabled tools are removed from the tool list sent to Gemini.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.enableCon: Stores conversation memory summaries when sessions end and enables transcript capture for memory generation. Increases API usage. Only recommended for paid tiers. AIs are still able to remember stuff mid conversation using the 'add_event_to_memory' tool
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.enableCit: Allows citizens to talk to each other without direct player speech input randomly.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.conversat: LIVE_WEBSOCKETS: Two Gemini Live sessions feed audio to each other in real time — cheap but lower quality. FLASH_TTS: Flash generates a script, then TTS renders multi-speaker audio — higher quality but ~10/day limit. AUTO (default): Tries Flash+TTS first, automatically falls back to Live WebSockets if the pipeline fails (e.g. quota exhausted).
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.enableRan: If enabled, nearby citizen pairs can start autonomous conversations at intervals. Requires citizen-to-citizen conversations to be enabled. Also players must not have muted their sound.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.randomCon: Per-check chance for an eligible nearby citizen to start a random citizen-to-citizen conversation.
How often the server checks for random citizen-to-citizen conversations. 20 ticks = 1 second.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.mumblingC: Per-check chance that an eligible nearby citizen starts mumbling to themselves.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.mumblingD: Base scan radius around players for mumbling and citizen-initiated contact. Random citizen conversations use twice this range.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.mumblingC: How often the server scans for mumbling opportunities near players. 20 ticks = 1 second.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.enableCit: Allows urgent citizens to proactively call out to nearby players without being prompted first.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.citizenCo: Base per-check chance for urgent contact. Multiplied by an urgency score from happiness, sickness, hunger, health, and homelessness.
How often the server checks whether urgent citizens should initiate contact. 20 ticks = 1 second.
Cooldown after an automatic session ends before that citizen can be auto-selected again (mumbling, urgent contact, or citizen-to-citizen). Set to 0 to disable.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.raidTraum: How long citizens express trauma in their prompts after a raid ends. During this window their speech reflects distress and memories of the attack. Set to 0 to disable.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.enablePer: If enabled, each citizen is randomly assigned a personality archetype (e.g. Optimist, Grump, Gossip) that persistently shapes how they speak.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.customPer: Freeform personality descriptions added to the assignment pool. Each entry is a plain-English instruction injected into the citizen's system prompt. Example: 'Always speak in rhyming couplets.'
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.enablePre: If true, enables pregeneration of citizen voices. That way AI will generate greetings / exclaims in the background and dynamically play them
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.pregenera: Distance within which pregenerated greetings can be played. If a citizen has a pregenerated greeting and you come within this distance, the greeting will play automatically without needing to start a conversation.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.threatPla: Minimum time in milliseconds between automatic threat audio plays for the same citizen. Set to 0 to disable.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.maxPregen: Maximum number of cached pregenerated greetings stored per citizen. Oldest greetings are discarded when this limit is reached.
yacl3.config.mc_talking:config.citizenVo: Marks the citizens voice channel as a whisper, which has a smaller default radius
The distance at which the citizen's voice can be heard. Only applies if the value is non 0.
